title: BACKND Base user signup and login flows
summary: Provides a routing overview for BACKND Base user entry flows, covering custom, guest, federation, and token-based login paths, plus the runtime fields typically populated after a successful login.
capabilities: route authentication questions to the correct login family
explain the difference between custom, guest, federation, and token login
describe what runtime user values become available after login
highlight token invalidation behavior when the same user signs in on another device
prerequisites: The selected login mode matches the game's account strategy
Platform tokens are available when federation login is used
common_pitfalls: Confusing signup routes with login-only routes
Assuming token login works before a token has been issued and stored locally
Ignoring invalid-token behavior after another device logs in with the same account
verification_required_for: exact runtime field names and examples
verification_strategy: Use https://docs.backnd.com plus the matching source route when the user asks for mutable provider, token, or API details.
Route provider-specific federation questions to the user-federation topic before answering.
public_notes: Agents should pick the specific child route that matches the requested account type instead of answering from the umbrella route alone.
Exact login method names, provider support, and implementation details should be rechecked from the official docs when producing code or current-state answers.
